items.title: They were Port Arthur's first victims. Thirty years on, their legacy continues

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

articles.title: Walter Mikac lost his wife and daughters at Port Arthur. Thirty years on, this is his message

children.content: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

image.altText: a bald man in a light blue t shirt

items.title: Fee to convert homes into short-stay accommodation jumps to $5,000

children.content: A Tasmanian council has pushed through a 1,050 per cent increase in fees for applications to turn a property into short-stay accommodation.

image.altText: Various houses on a hill in Hobart

items.title: Influencers to pay $100 to make money off Tasmania's national parks

children.content: Influencers and content creators will be able to film and profit from content gathered in Tasmania's national parks for just $100, under new regulations.

image.altText: Unidentified female backpacker looks out to sea from rugged coastline.

items.title: Who's who in Tasmania's upper house elections this weekend

children.content: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

image.altText: Six cards with candidate faces on them.

items.title: When this mum's rental ended, she and her two teenagers moved into their car

children.content: An analysis of 12 months of urgent housing and homelessness attendances in Tasmania has shown an alarming spike in people with dependent children needing help — while overcrowding is an increasing concern.

image.altText: A mother with her head in her hands sits in the driver's seat of a car while two kids sit in the back under a blanket.

items.title: This week could see Tasmania's hottest May day in nearly 140 years

children.content: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

gallery.altText: The sun looks like a glowing yellow-white ball in an orange sky above trees that are silhouetted brown-black.

thumbnail.altText: The sun looks like a glowing yellow-white ball in an orange sky above trees that are silhouetted brown-black.

items.title: One of Australia's iconic tropical islands now costs twice as much to run

items.title: Paralympic champion shares emotional volatility after traumatic brain injury

children.content: Alexa Leary sustained a traumatic brain injury (TBI) after a cycling accident at age 19. The two-time Paralympic gold medallist turned author is calling for a day to recognise TBI and the unique challenges it brings.
